name: Design System (DS) Ontology
description: End-to-end design system ontology modelling the three-tier token cascade (Primitive → Semantic → Component), Figma source integration, multi-brand variant support, atomic design patterns, and theme mode management. Bridges to EFS for feature realisation, EMC for PFI instance configuration and brand resolution, and PE for design workflow governance. Aligned with W3C Design Tokens Community Group specification and Brad Frost's Atomic Design methodology.

oaa:imports.name: EFS-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Enterprise Framework Services providing Feature entity for design component to feature realisation mapping

oaa:imports.name: EMC-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Enterprise Model Composition providing InstanceConfiguration for PFI-driven brand and design system resolution

oaa:imports.name: PE-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Process Engineering providing Process entity for design workflow governance

oaa:imports.name: ORG-CONTEXT-ONT
oaa:imports.description: Organization Context providing Brand entity for design system to organizational brand correlation

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Design Component Feature Realisation Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Design components realise EFS features, connecting UI implementation to agile delivery backlog items
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Trace which UI components implement which product features

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Design System PFI Configuration Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: EMC instance configuration determines which design system, brand variant, and Figma source to load for a given PFI product
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: PFI-driven brand resolution: EMC tells which DS config URL, Figma file key, and tokens to load

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Design Workflow Process Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Design system extraction, validation, and deployment governed by PE process engineering workflows
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Govern the Figma → Extract → Validate → Store → Render pipeline via PE process

oaa:joinPatterns.name: Organization to Figma Traceability Bridge
oaa:joinPatterns.description: Correlate organizations to design systems and Figma source files via brand ownership
oaa:joinPatterns.joinPath: org-ctx:Brand → ds:ownedByBrand → ds:DesignSystem → ds:hasVariant → ds:BrandVariant → ds:hasFigmaSource → ds:FigmaSource
oaa:joinPatterns.useCase: Trace from any org to its design systems, brand variants, and Figma source URLs

entities.name: DesignSystem
entities.description: Root container for a design system definition. Encapsulates the token taxonomy (primitives, semantics, components), UI components, brand variants, theme modes, and Figma source bindings. Each PFI product instance resolves to one active DesignSystem via EMC InstanceConfiguration. Aligned with W3C Design Tokens Community Group specification.

oaa:properties.name: systemId
oaa:properties.description: Unique identifier for the design system

oaa:properties.name: name
oaa:properties.description: Human-readable name (e.g., 'BAIV Design System')

oaa:properties.name: version
oaa:properties.description: Semantic version of the design system

oaa:properties.name: namespace
oaa:properties.description: Namespace URI for this design system's tokens

oaa:properties.name: themeModeSupport
oaa:properties.description: Whether the design system supports light/dark theme modes

oaa:properties.name: configUrl
oaa:properties.description: URL to the published DS configuration JSON file for runtime resolution

oaa:properties.name: isActive
oaa:properties.description: Whether this is the currently active design system (only one active per context)

oaa:properties.name: pfiInstanceName
oaa:properties.description: PF-Instance identifier this design system belongs to (e.g. PFI-VHF, PFI-BAIV). Enables filtering without traversing EMC.

entities.name: TokenCategory
entities.description: A grouping of design tokens by type or intent. At the primitive tier: colors, spacing, radius, typography, shadows. At the semantic tier: surface, text, border, accent, feedback. At the component tier: grouped by component name (button, card, input, etc.). Aligned with W3C Design Tokens $type grouping.

oaa:properties.name: categoryName
oaa:properties.description: Category identifier (e.g., 'colors', 'spacing', 'surface', 'button')

oaa:properties.name: tier
oaa:properties.description: Which tier this category belongs to (Primitive, Semantic, or Component)

oaa:properties.name: defaultMutabilityTier
oaa:properties.description: Default mutability classification for tokens in this category. Spacing and radius categories default to PF-Core; colour categories default to PF-Instance. Individual tokens may override this default.

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: Purpose and scope of this token category

entities.name: PrimitiveToken
entities.description: A raw, context-free design value at the base tier of the token cascade. Primitives define the foundational palette: colour scales (e.g., blue-50 through blue-950), spacing units (xs through 3xl), border radii, typography values (font families, sizes, weights, line heights), and shadows. Aligned with W3C Design Tokens $value specification.

oaa:properties.name: tokenName
oaa:properties.description: Dot-notation token path (e.g., 'color.blue.500', 'spacing.md')

oaa:properties.name: value
oaa:properties.description: Raw CSS value (e.g., 'hsl(217 91% 60%)', '1rem', '0.5rem')

oaa:properties.name: valueType
oaa:properties.description: Type classification of the token value

oaa:properties.name: mutabilityTier
oaa:properties.description: Whether this token is structurally immutable (PF-Core) or brand-variable (PF-Instance). Spacing and radius primitives are PF-Core; colour primitives are PF-Instance.

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: Human-readable description of the primitive

entities.name: SemanticToken
entities.description: An intent-based design token at the middle tier of the cascade. Semantic tokens convey design purpose rather than raw values, referencing primitives via the $ref/alias pattern. Examples: text.primary → color.neutral.900, surface.default → color.neutral.50, accent.primary → color.teal.600. Each semantic token can have light and dark mode variants. Aligned with W3C Design Tokens alias specification.

oaa:properties.name: tokenName
oaa:properties.description: Intent-based token path (e.g., 'text.primary', 'surface.default', 'accent.primary')

oaa:properties.name: lightModeValue
oaa:properties.description: Resolved value or primitive reference for light mode

oaa:properties.name: darkModeValue
oaa:properties.description: Resolved value or primitive reference for dark mode

oaa:properties.name: mutabilityTier
oaa:properties.description: Whether this token is structurally immutable (PF-Core) or brand-variable (PF-Instance). All semantic tokens are colour-based and therefore PF-Instance.

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: Design intent description

entities.name: ComponentToken
entities.description: A component-specific design token at the top tier of the cascade. Component tokens bind to specific UI component parts and states, referencing semantic tokens for consistency. Examples: button.primary.background → accent.primary, card.default.border → border.subtle, input.focus.ring → accent.primaryHover. Enables component-level theming without direct primitive access.

oaa:properties.name: tokenName
oaa:properties.description: Component-scoped token path (e.g., 'button.primary.background')

oaa:properties.name: componentName
oaa:properties.description: Target component (e.g., 'button', 'card', 'input')

oaa:properties.name: partOrState
oaa:properties.description: Component part or interaction state (e.g., 'background', 'hover', 'disabled', 'focusRing')

oaa:properties.name: mutabilityTier
oaa:properties.description: Whether this token is structurally immutable (PF-Core) or brand-variable (PF-Instance). Dimensional tokens (radius, height, width, padding) are PF-Core; colour tokens are PF-Instance.

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: Usage context for this component token

entities.name: DesignComponent
entities.description: A UI component definition within the design system, classified by atomic design level (atom, molecule, organism, template, page). Components consume component tokens and can be generated as React/CSS code for Shadcn/ui integration or as Figma Make input. Components may optionally allow token overrides for contextual customisation.

oaa:properties.name: componentName
oaa:properties.description: Component identifier (e.g., 'Button', 'Card', 'InputGroup')

oaa:properties.name: category
oaa:properties.description: UI category (e.g., 'Form', 'Feedback', 'Layout', 'Navigation', 'Data')

oaa:properties.name: baseComponent
oaa:properties.description: Underlying framework component (e.g., 'shadcn/ui Button', 'radix Checkbox')

oaa:properties.name: allowsOverrides
oaa:properties.description: Whether the component accepts tokenOverrides prop for contextual customisation

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: Component purpose and usage guidelines

entities.name: BrandVariant
entities.description: A brand-specific configuration within the design system. Each PFI product (BAIV, W4M, co-branded clients) has a BrandVariant that binds to a Figma source file and may override tokens at any tier. Brand switching is achieved by swapping the active BrandVariant, which changes the Figma source URL and token overrides while preserving the component architecture.

oaa:properties.name: brandId
oaa:properties.description: Unique brand identifier (e.g., 'baiv', 'w4m', 'azlan-pfc')

oaa:properties.name: brandName
oaa:properties.description: Human-readable brand name (e.g., 'Be AI Visible', 'Work4Me')

oaa:properties.name: isActive
oaa:properties.description: Whether this brand variant is currently active

oaa:properties.name: tokenOverrides
oaa:properties.description: JSON path to brand-specific token overrides file or inline overrides

oaa:properties.name: overridableScope
oaa:properties.description: Which mutability tier this variant is permitted to override. Must be PF-Instance — brands cannot override PF-Core structural tokens (spacing, radius, type scale, component dimensions).

oaa:properties.name: namespace
oaa:properties.description: Brand-specific namespace for token scoping

entities.name: FigmaSource
entities.description: Figma file source metadata for design token extraction. Each BrandVariant binds to one FigmaSource specifying the Figma file key, file name, variable collections, and sync state. Extraction is performed via Figma MCP tools (get_variable_defs, get_design_context, get_screenshot). Sync staleness tracked to ensure tokens stay current with design.

oaa:properties.name: fileKey
oaa:properties.description: Figma file key identifier (e.g., 'bXCyfNwzc8Z9kEeFIeIB8C')

oaa:properties.name: fileName
oaa:properties.description: Human-readable Figma file name (e.g., 'BAIV Figma Variables v3')

oaa:properties.name: fileUrl
oaa:properties.description: Full Figma file URL for MCP access

oaa:properties.name: lastSyncedAt
oaa:properties.description: Timestamp of last successful token extraction sync

oaa:properties.name: syncStatus
oaa:properties.description: Current sync state (Synced, Stale, NeverSynced, Error)

oaa:properties.name: variableCollections
oaa:properties.description: Comma-separated list of Figma variable collection names to extract

entities.name: ThemeMode
entities.description: A theme mode configuration specifying how semantic tokens resolve for a given display context. Standard modes are Light and Dark (mapped to CSS prefers-color-scheme). System mode auto-detects from OS settings. HighContrast mode supports accessibility requirements. Each mode defines alternate token resolution paths.

oaa:properties.name: modeType
oaa:properties.description: Mode type (Light, Dark, System, HighContrast)

oaa:properties.name: isDefault
oaa:properties.description: Whether this is the default mode when no preference is detected

oaa:properties.name: cssMediaQuery
oaa:properties.description: CSS media query that activates this mode (e.g., '(prefers-color-scheme: dark)')

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: Mode purpose and behaviour description

entities.name: DesignPattern
entities.description: An atomic design classification level defining the complexity hierarchy of UI components. Follows Brad Frost's Atomic Design methodology: Atoms (basic HTML elements, single tokens), Molecules (simple component groups), Organisms (complex UI sections), Templates (page-level wireframes), Pages (specific instances with real content). Used to classify DesignComponents and govern composition rules.

oaa:properties.name: level
oaa:properties.description: Atomic design level classification

oaa:properties.name: name
oaa:properties.description: Pattern name (e.g., 'Form Field Group', 'Navigation Bar', 'Dashboard Layout')

oaa:properties.name: composableFrom
oaa:properties.description: Comma-separated list of lower-level patterns this pattern can compose from

oaa:properties.name: description
oaa:properties.description: Pattern usage guidelines and composition rules

entities.name: Feature
entities.description: External: EFS Feature — distinct functionality delivering user or business value, decomposable into user stories. Design components realise features.

entities.name: InstanceConfiguration
entities.description: External: EMC PFI Instance Configuration — pre-defined configuration specifying which ontologies, design system, and instance data to load for a given PFI product.

entities.name: Process
entities.description: External: PE Process — a defined sequence of activities and gates governing how work progresses. Design system extraction/validation/deployment follows PE process workflows.

entities.name: Brand
entities.description: External: ORG-CONTEXT Brand — organizational brand carrying market identity and value proposition. Design systems bind to brands for visual identity governance.

relationships.name: hasTokenCategory
relationships.description: Design system organises tokens into categories by type and tier

relationships.name: hasPrimitive
relationships.description: Token category contains primitive tokens (raw design values)

relationships.name: referencesPrimitive
relationships.description: Semantic token references a primitive token for its resolved value — the core cascade mechanism. Aligned with W3C Design Tokens $ref/alias pattern.

relationships.name: referencesSemantic
relationships.description: Component token references a semantic token for design-intent consistency — second level of the cascade mechanism

relationships.name: hasComponent
relationships.description: Design system contains UI component definitions

relationships.name: consumesTokens
relationships.description: Design component consumes component tokens for its visual properties (background, border, text, spacing, etc.)

relationships.name: hasVariant
relationships.description: Design system supports multiple brand variants (BAIV, W4M, co-branded clients)

relationships.name: hasFigmaSource
relationships.description: Brand variant binds to a Figma file as single source of truth for design tokens

relationships.name: supportsMode
relationships.description: Design system supports theme modes (light, dark, system, high-contrast)

relationships.name: hasDesignLevel
relationships.description: Design component is classified at an atomic design level (atom, molecule, organism, template, page)

relationships.name: realizesFeature
relationships.description: Design component realises an EFS feature specification, connecting UI implementation to agile delivery backlog

relationships.name: configuredByInstance
relationships.description: Design system configuration is determined by EMC PFI instance configuration — specifying which brand, Figma source, and token file URL to load

relationships.name: governedByProcess
relationships.description: Design system extraction, validation, and deployment workflow governed by PE process engineering

relationships.name: ownedByBrand
relationships.description: Design system is owned by an organizational brand, correlating visual identity to organizational structure

enumerations.name: MutabilityTier
enumerations.description: Token mutability classification for brand override governance. PF-Core tokens are structurally immutable across all brands (spacing, radius, typography scale, component dimensions). PF-Instance tokens are brand-variable (colours, font families, semantic colour values).

enumerations.name: AtomicDesignLevel
enumerations.description: Brad Frost's Atomic Design component complexity hierarchy

enumerations.name: ThemeModeType
enumerations.description: Supported theme mode types mapped to CSS prefers-color-scheme and accessibility modes

enumerations.name: TokenValueType
enumerations.description: W3C Design Tokens $type classification for primitive token values

businessRules.name: SemanticMustReferencePrimitive
businessRules.description: Semantic tokens must reference a primitive token to maintain the cascade
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one referencesPrimitive relationship

businessRules.name: ComponentMustReferenceSemantic
businessRules.description: Component tokens must reference a semantic token to maintain design intent
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one referencesSemantic relationship

businessRules.name: ConsumedTokensMustExist
businessRules.description: All component tokens consumed by a design component must be defined
businessRules.action: THEN all referenced ComponentTokens MUST exist in the design system

businessRules.name: ThemeModeCompleteness
businessRules.description: When theme mode support is enabled, all semantic tokens must define both light and dark mode values
businessRules.action: THEN all SemanticTokens MUST have both lightModeValue AND darkModeValue defined

businessRules.name: FigmaSyncStaleness
businessRules.description: Figma sources older than 7 days should be flagged as stale
businessRules.condition: IF FigmaSource.lastSyncedAt is older than 7 days
businessRules.action: THEN syncStatus MUST be set to 'Stale'

businessRules.name: BrandMustHaveFigmaSource
businessRules.description: Every brand variant must bind to a Figma source file
businessRules.action: THEN it MUST have exactly one hasFigmaSource relationship

businessRules.name: ComponentShouldRealiseFeature
businessRules.description: Design components should trace to EFS features for backlog traceability
businessRules.action: THEN it SHOULD have at least one realizesFeature relationship

businessRules.name: InstanceMustSpecifyBrand
businessRules.description: When configured by an EMC instance, the instance must specify a valid brand variant
businessRules.action: THEN the referenced InstanceConfiguration MUST specify a valid brandId

businessRules.name: BrandOverrideImmutability
businessRules.description: Brand variant token overrides must only target PF-Instance tokens. PF-Core tokens (spacing, radius, typography scale, component dimensions) are structurally immutable and must remain consistent across all brands to preserve layout integrity.
businessRules.condition: IF BrandVariant.tokenOverrides contains a token reference
businessRules.action: THEN the referenced token.mutabilityTier MUST equal 'PF-Instance'

crossOntologyReferences.description: DS-ONT bridges to three ontologies: EFS for feature realisation (connecting UI components to agile backlog), EMC for PFI instance-driven brand and config resolution, and PE for design workflow process governance.

keyBridges.purpose: Trace UI component implementation to product feature specifications
PFI-driven brand resolution: which DS config URL, Figma key, and tokens to load for BAIV/W4M/etc.
Figma → Extract → Validate → Store → Render pipeline governance
Design system to organizational brand correlation

existingExternalRefs.description: EMC instances will reference DS configs as part of PFI product composition (future EMC update)
